visual_features.overall_style: Clean but somewhat dated informational layout with a traditional, church-appropriate tone. Visual style is mostly minimalist and content-first, using flat design with almost no shadows or gradients. The large photographic header provides warmth and context while the rest of the page is very utilitarian.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by size, position, and color rather than bold decoration. The full‑width hero photo is the first focal point, followed by the large dark-grey page title. The sidebar section menu is visually secondary but clear due to its column placement and horizontal rules. Within the main content, a colored first paragraph in green draws attention after the heading. Navigation links in the global header are small and understated, indicating tertiary priority.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderate whitespace with clear separation between global header, hero image, sidebar, and main content. The layout is not cramped, but margins and paddings are relatively conservative (more functional than airy). The main content column has adequate left/right padding, while the sidebar relies heavily on line separators rather than large padding gaps.
unique_elements: Full-width hero photograph of the church directly beneath the top navigation, spanning entire viewport width.
Left-hand contextual section menu with thin horizontal rules and a grey highlight for the active item.
Green brand color reused in logo, active sidebar link, and introductory paragraph text for conceptual consistency.
Small RSS badge icon preceding the page heading as a legacy content-subscription cue.
Small illustration combining a cross, dove, and water as a decorative faith-related icon within the content.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit two-column layout likely based on a 12-column grid, with approximately 3–4 columns for the left sidebar and 8–9 columns for the main content area within a centered max-width container.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Standard top navigation + hero + two-column content layout. Global nav and logo at the top, full-width hero image below, then a left sidebar navigation alongside a right main content column.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top-to-bottom structure: (1) thin top bar with logo and horizontal menu, (2) full-width hero image, (3) main content band containing a left vertical section menu and a right article area (title, RSS badge, text, illustration). Content appears to continue downward as a standard article page.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall left-aligned content with a centered global header container. Sidebar items are left-aligned with equal indenting; main content text and headings are left-aligned. Navigation links along the top are arranged in a single horizontal line with even spacing.
responsive_hints: Full-width hero suggests it can scale fluidly on different screen widths.
Two-column layout (sidebar + content) is likely to stack into a single column on smaller screens, with sidebar either preceding or following main content.
Top navigation is text-based and may collapse into a stacked or hamburger menu on narrow viewports (not visible but consistent with typical implementations).
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: top global nav, then a full-width photographic hero. Below the hero, content sits in a centered container split into two columns. The left sidebar is a vertical list of section links separated by thin horizontal rules; one item is highlighted to show the current page. The right column starts with a small inline RSS badge followed by a large page title, then a body section where the first paragraph is highlighted in green text and an illustrative icon is positioned toward the right side of the text area.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The hero image occupies the full width and about 25–30% of the visible height. Below, the main content band uses most of the remaining vertical space. Horizontally, the sidebar takes roughly 25–30% of the content width, while the main article area uses 70–75%. Vertical spacing between sidebar items is modest (relying on single-line height plus line separators), while vertical spacing around the main heading and between heading and body text is more generous, giving emphasis to the title.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderate content density. The sidebar is relatively dense with closely stacked links, while the main content column is more spacious with clear separation between heading, intro paragraph, and subsequent content. There is not an abundance of white margins, but neither is the page cluttered: it feels like a traditional informational site with utilitarian spacing.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye follows a mostly vertical flow. Users first see the logo and global nav across the top, then are drawn down to the large hero image. From there, the eye moves slightly right to the large page heading in the main column. Once the title is read, attention flows downward to the green introductory text and then to the illustration on the right. The left sidebar is discovered either immediately after the heading (due to column structure) or via peripheral scanning, enabling movement between related pages.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No prominent traditional buttons are visible in the screenshot; actions are mostly text links. Where button-like elements would exist (e.g., for forms or CTAs), they would likely be flat rectangular shapes with minimal corner rounding (2–4px radius), solid fill (brand green or light grey), no shadows, and simple 1px borders in a slightly darker shade. Text would be center-aligned, medium weight sans-serif, likely in white on a green background or dark grey on a light-grey background.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Buttons are likely implemented as elements styled as buttons or standard

ui_patterns.visual_description: There are no explicit material-style cards; instead, the layout uses flat content blocks. If treated as cards, each block would have a white background, thin grey border (#CCCCCC), no or very subtle shadow, and 12–16px internal padding. Corners would be square or very slightly rounded (2px). Separation between blocks would be provided by margin rather than heavy decoration.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
A typical card pattern would be a simple bordered div containing a heading, optional meta information (like an RSS or icon badge), and body text:

Title

Content...

. Where images are used, they appear above or beside text without overlays.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Forms are not in view, but based on the site style they would likely feature full-width text inputs with white backgrounds, 1px grey borders, and square corners. Labels would be placed above inputs, left-aligned, with small-to-regular font size. Focus states may darken the border or change it to the brand green. Validation feedback would likely appear as small red or green text beneath fields without complex iconography.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Stacked vertical form layout with each field in its own block:
. Fieldsets or simple headings may group related inputs. Submit buttons likely appear below the form as a single primary action.

ui_patterns.visual_description:
Global navigation: horizontal text menu aligned to the right of the logo, on a white background with no distinct nav bar color. Menu items are simple text links (dark grey/black), evenly spaced with generous horizontal padding. Active or hovered items may show an underline or subtle color change. Dropdown indicators (not visible but implied by 'About Us ▼', 'News ▼', etc.) are likely small arrows next to text.
Sidebar navigation: vertical list with each item spanning the width of the sidebar. Items are separated by thin grey horizontal lines. The active item has a light grey background block (#E5E5E5) with green text, creating a clear current-location highlight. Text is left-aligned with minimal left padding.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Global navigation likely uses a semantic unordered list:
  • Home
    • ...
. Dropdowns would be nested
    elements inside
  • . Sidebar navigation is similarly an unordered list with list-style removed and custom borders applied between
  • items.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The sidebar itself is a key list pattern: a single column of items with moderate density, each separated by a light 1px horizontal rule. Text is standard-weight sans-serif, with increased contrast when active via background and color change. The list feels menu-like rather than content-like. In content areas, lists (not visible but implied) would likely be simple bullet lists with standard indentation and no custom markers.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Sidebar implemented as
  • Item
    • ...
. Content lists as standard
    /
      with minimal styling. More complex list layouts (e.g., article teasers) would be flexbox-based media objects: icon or thumbnail on left, text on right, all inside a list item or div.

heading.visual_style: Headings appear to use a neutral sans-serif with relatively standard proportions (not condensed, moderate x-height) and medium-to-bold weight, giving a straightforward, informational tone rather than decorative or highly modern.

body.visual_style: Body text looks like a clean, highly readable sans-serif (similar to Arial/Helvetica) with regular weight and standard character width, prioritizing legibility over personality.

spacing_system.component_padding: Most content blocks and navigation items appear to use 8–16px padding horizontally and vertically (e.g., 12–16px above/below sidebar links, similar values around main content text).
spacing_system.section_margin: Major sections (e.g., between hero image and content band, or between heading and body text) use roughly 24–32px vertical margins to create clear separation without feeling overly spacious.

interaction_hints: Text links in nav and sidebar likely change color (e.g., darker grey or green) and/or gain underlines on hover. The sidebar active item already shows a persistent state via background and text color change.

Global navigation dropdowns (for 'About Us', 'News', 'Services and Prayer') probably reveal simple vertical lists on hover or click, with the same text-link styling and minimal animation (instant or very fast fade).
Buttons and interactive elements would benefit from subtle transitions: 150–200ms color and background transitions, possibly slight brightness or border-color shifts on hover/focus.
Sidebar items might gain a slightly darker grey background on hover to reinforce clickability, with cursor: pointer on links.
Illustrative icons (like the RSS badge) are static; no animated micro-interactions are visible, suggesting a low-motion, accessibility-friendly environment.
